extraction.one_line_summary: VIÉMOËS Alexis won from 6-rider breakaway that held off peloton by 5 seconds after sprint teams completely misjudged closing speed inside final kilometer.
tactical_patterns: Ineos controlled all day with Hamilton but six-rider break still survived, suggesting miscalculated threshold watts on rolling Ardèche terrain.
LE GAC attacked right side inside 600m despite being in break, forcing Viémoës to respond and gapping others before sprint.
Peloton led by WVA closed 21 seconds to 5 seconds in final 1500m but started chase too late to catch organized breakaway.

rider_signals.VIÉMOËS Alexis: positive: First win in 3+ years, showed tactical patience in break finale after LE GAC's late attack forced response
rider_signals.LE GAC Olivier: negative: Had freshest legs in break finale but attacked too early at 600m, allowing Viémoës to react and counter-sprint
rider_signals.WOUT VAN AERT: neutral: Led peloton chase but started sprint effort too late, suggesting team miscalculated break's residual strength on rolling finish
rider_signals.VERVLOESEM Andres: negative: Dropped from break on Col de Mésclac despite only 4.1% gradient, indicating insufficient climbing form
